title: Tafanda Bay
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}} {{Citation}} {{City |image=[[File:IthorianHerdShip-FF122.png]] |name=''Tafanda Bay'' |constructed= |destroyed= |rebuilt= |builder=[[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorians]] |mayor= |celestialbody=[[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]] |continent= |location= |climate=[[Temperature/Legends|Warm]] |interest=*Upper and lower observation decks *Market places *[[Great Atrium]] *[[Botanical Dome]]s |population= |demonym= |affiliation=*[[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorians]] **[[Ithorian ground fleet]] }} {{Quote|Is that a city?''"
"''More than just a city, an entire enclosed environment. The Ithorians call it the ''Tafanda Bay''.|Qwi Xux and Wedge Antilles|[[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']]}} '''''Tafanda Bay''''' was the name of a floating [[Ithorian Herdship|herdship]] [[City/Legends|city]] above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]]. [[Momaw Nadon/Legends|Momaw Nadon]] was [[High Priest/Legends|high priest]] of this city during Imperial intervention. Later, during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], [[Relal Tawron]] served as high priest. ==Characteristics== [[File:Tafanda.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Tafanda Bay [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]] [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] herd ships were hundreds of [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall and could house several thousand inhabitants.The ships hovered above the surface of Ithor, and the Ithorians considered the jungles within them sacred and would not allow themselves—or others—to set foot in them. The Ithorians lived on these herd ships for thousands of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] and pointed to them as examples of ways to integrate technology and nature harmoniously. Unlike the gleaming towers preferred by [[Human/Legends|Humans]], the ''Tafanda Bay''{{'s}} exterior was covered by mosses, flowers, and immense [[Tree/Legends|trees]]. The ''Tafanda Bay'' had landing platforms for incoming [[Starship/Legends|starships]] and [[Airspeeder/Legends|speeders]], while dozens of gigantic [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] engines propelled it slowly over the jungle landscape. The ship's interior environments reproduced virtually every terrain known to Ithor as well as many found on other worlds. The ship had several large halls where visitors could purchase goods from the shrewd Ithorian traders. A popular area to visit was the Great Atrium; it was nearly a [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] across, with moss-covered walls leading to the open air above. Other popular destinations included the upper and lower observation decks, which offered stunning views of the jungle and Ithor's brilliant violet night sky. Adventurous visitors were allowed to rent speeders for guided sight-seeing tours above the jungle. ''Tafanda Bay'' was the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the [[Ithorian ground fleet]]. ==History== {{Quote|Look out there, Jacen. This is a city that is now abandoned by the people who love it, who labored to create it. Why? Because it's a target. We know the Vong are going to hit it, so we've moved the people and have rigged up some surprises for the enemy.|Corran Horn|''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin]]''}} The majestic eco-city was a popular destination for tourists and a center of commerce in the [[Lesser Plooriod Cluster/Legends|Lesser Plooriod Cluster]].''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' During the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor Palpatine]] sent the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Conquest (Imperial-class)/Legends|''Conquest'']] to persuade the Ithorians to give up their ecological secrets. [[Momaw Nadon/Legends|Momaw Nadon]], [[High Priest/Legends|high priest]] of ''Tafanda Bay'' at the time, surrendered the knowledge gathered together by generations of Ithorian botanists so that no harm would come to the jungles of Ithor. The Ithorians banished Momaw to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] to serve his sentence. After the next "great meet" of Ithorian Herd Ships, Momaw returned to his place as high priest of ''Tafanda Bay''. Years later, during the war with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala|Daala]], [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and alien scientist [[Qwi Xux]] arrived on ''Tafanda Bay'' hoping that Momaw Nadon would protect Qwi and her knowledge from the forces of the Empire. The loyalty of the Ithorians wasn't enough though to prevent [[Kyp Durron]], under the influence of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]], from erasing Qwi's mind. In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], Relal Towron replaced Momaw Nadon as high priest of ''Tafanda Bay''. The Yuuzhan Vong invaded ''Tafanda Bay'' during the [[Battle of Ithor]]. The ''[[Legacy of Torment]]'' arrived in the system above Ithor after [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] discovered on [[Garqi/Legends|Garqi]] that the [[Bafforr tree]]s reacted violently with the Yuuzhan Vong armor. As [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Admiral Gilad Pellaeon]], [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], and a force of [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] aliens led by [[Jagged Fel]] engaged [[Yorik-et|Coralskippers]] in space, the [[Yorik-trema]] landed their troops inside ''Tafanda Bay''. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and his [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] allies engaged the Yuuzhan Vong and [[Chazrach]] aboard the herd ship. Relal Towron had to be evacuated to the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] after the invasion. Ithor was destroyed in this battle, presumably taking ''Tafanda Bay'' with it. ==Appearances== *{{StoryCite|story=The Sand Tender: The Hammerhead's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}} {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars Missions 6: The Search for Grubba the Hutt]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars Missions 7: Ithorian Invasion]]'' *''[[Galaxy of Fear: Spore]]'' *[[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']] {{1st}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Tafanda Bay}} *''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]'' *''[[Galaxy Guide 7: Mos Eisley]]'' *''[[The Movie Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' *[[Star Wars Screen Entertainment|''Star Wars'' Screen Entertainment]] *[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope'', Second Edition]] *''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' *''[[Star Wars Trilogy Sourcebook, Special Edition]]'' *{{Kids1997Cite|8|Who Is That Alien?|Who Is That Alien? Ithorians & Gamorreans}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' *{{GamerCite|4|Starfaring Jungles}} *''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' *{{FactFile|33|CAN 7-10|Mos Eisley Cantina Characters}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 36|''Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection'' 36]] *{{DB|character|momawnadon|Nadon, Momaw}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Tafanda Bay |de=Tafanda Bay |es=Bahía de Tafanda |nl=Tafanda Bay }} [[Category:Herdships]] [[Category:Ithor locations]]
